Improve hook reliability and session status tracking (#44)

* fix: improve hook reliability and leverage new Claude Code features

- Add async: true to all hooks (fire-and-forget, no blocking Claude Code)
- Add SessionEnd hook for instant session cleanup on exit
- Add PostToolUseFailure hook to track tool errors and avoid stuck "working"
- Map elicitation_dialog notification to waitingInput (was falling through)
- Expand SessionStart matcher to cover clear and compact triggers
- Change Stop transition from idle to waitingInput (session IS waiting)
- Refactor HookHandler to fix lint violations (extract applyTransition,
  move extractToolDetail to file scope)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect suspended (Ctrl+Z) processes as dead sessions

Processes in SSTOP state (p_stat == 4) will never fire hooks again
unless resumed. Treat them as dead so the menubar app cleans them up
instead of showing zombie sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: decay waitingInput to idle after 60 minutes of inactivity

Display-side adjustment only — session files are not modified. If a
session has been in waitingInput for over an hour, the user likely
walked away, so show it as idle instead.

commit.message:
feat(phase7.5): Wave 5 -- Crypto & Protocols (v0.13.0)

Add 7 security and application protocol modules (~14,500 lines, 247 tests):

- TLS 1.3 (tls.rs, ~3,360 lines, 34 tests): X25519 key exchange with
  5-limb field arithmetic, ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD with 5-limb Poly1305
  MAC, HKDF-SHA256, record layer, session resumption, 0-RTT early data
- SSH-2.0 Server (ssh.rs, ~2,680 lines, 51 tests): binary packet protocol,
  curve25519-sha256 KEX, Ed25519 host key, password/publickey auth,
  channel multiplexing with flow control, PTY allocation, shell/exec
- HTTP/1.1 Client (http.rs, ~1,360 lines, 28 tests): request builder,
  chunked transfer-encoding, redirect following, URL parser, keep-alive
- NTP Client (ntp.rs, ~1,120 lines, 25 tests): NTPv4 packet format,
  clock filter algorithm, drift estimator, poll interval adjustment,
  boot-time RTC sync
- QUIC Transport (quic.rs, ~2,710 lines, 44 tests): RFC 9000 varint
  encoding, long/short headers, connection ID routing, stream multiplexing,
  connection state machine, loss detection stubs
- WireGuard VPN (wireguard.rs, ~1,670 lines, 36 tests): Noise IK handshake,
  BLAKE2s (10-round), HMAC-BLAKE2s, anti-replay window, keepalive timers,
  tunnel encapsulation/decapsulation
- mDNS/DNS-SD (mdns.rs, ~1,650 lines, 29 tests): RFC 6762/6763 multicast
  DNS, service registration/discovery, probe-announce state machine,
  cache with TTL expiry, TXT record encoding

All 4 clippy targets clean (0 warnings). All 3 architectures build.
1,742 host-target unit tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Log invite email API errors instead of silently swallowing them

fetch() doesn't reject on HTTP errors (400/500), so the catch block
never fires. Now checks response.ok and logs the actual error body
to the console for debugging.

commit.message:
Update etwmon to support Windows 11 (#1867)

* Update etwmon to support Windows 11

Windows 11 is missing EtwAdminlessProvRegHandle and IoMgrTraceHandle. This allows them to just be skipped but still process

* etwmon: Add Windows 11 global handle list

Windows 11 (build >= 22000) removed EtwAdminlessProvRegHandle and
IoMgrTraceHandle, and added 8 new handles: EtwpDiskProvRegHandle,
EtwCpuPartitionProvRegHandle, EtwCpuStarvationProvRegHandle,
IopDumpEtwRegHandle, PnpEtwHandle, PnpRundownEtwHandle,
WheapEtwHandle, and SshpTraceHandle.

Add a dedicated win11_global_handles list and select it when the
build number indicates Windows 11. Restore throw on unresolved
symbols since each list now contains only handles known to exist
on its target OS version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve choropleth annotations: larger font + contrast-adaptive colour

- Increase annotation font size from 8 → 12 (50% larger)
- Add WCAG-compliant per-point text colour: dark (#111827) on light
  fills, white (#f9fafb) on dark fills, switching at luminance ≈ 0.179
- Hardcoded colorscale stop tables (YlOrRd, Greens, Blues, Oranges,
  Viridis, RdBu) drive the contrast computation via linear interpolation
- Remove single static textColor; annotTrace.textfont.color is now
  a per-point array

commit.message:
FIX: Comprehensive fix for uneven Key Strengths/Risk Factors alignment

Complete restructure of the two-column layout to ensure perfect alignment:

1. **CSS Grid with auto-rows-fr**:
   - Changed from flexbox to CSS Grid with `md:auto-rows-fr`
   - This forces all grid rows to be equal fractional height
   - Ensures both boxes are EXACTLY the same height on desktop

2. **Nested Flexbox for Content**:
   - Each box uses `flex flex-col` to create vertical flex container
   - Header has `flex-shrink-0` to prevent compression
   - Content wrapper has `flex-grow` to fill available space
   - This distributes space correctly inside each box

3. **Overflow Prevention**:
   - Added `min-h-0` to both boxes (flex item overflow fix)
   - Added `flex-shrink-0` to SVG icons to prevent squashing
   - Ensures content doesn't break the grid layout

4. **Mobile Stacking**:
   - `grid-cols-1` on mobile for vertical stack
   - `md:grid-cols-2` on desktop for side-by-side

This approach combines the best of both Grid (for equal heights) and
Flexbox (for content distribution within each box).

Location: components/valuation/ValuationResults.tsx:248-303

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat: replace manager nudge cycle with auditor spawn (#495)

* feat: replace manager nudge cycle with auditor spawn for stuck stories

When auditor_enabled is true (default), the manager now spawns an
auditor agent instead of running nudgeStuckStories() directly. The
auditor lifecycle checks interval timing and prevents duplicate spawns
by querying for active auditor agents. When auditor_enabled is false,
the existing nudge behavior is preserved for backwards compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 銘柄削除 '[object Object]' error — remove conflicting DELETE endpoint

- Remove old DELETE /securities/{security_id} (int-typed) endpoint that was
  registered before the new DELETE /securities/{code} (str-typed) endpoint.
  FastAPI matched Japanese-encoded codes against the int route first, returning
  a 422 with detail as an array → 'new Error(array)' → '[object Object]'.

- Fix JS error handling in deleteSecurity() to stringify non-string detail
  values (typeof check + JSON.stringify fallback).

- Add filterDeleteCandidates() with inline search: typing in the delete
  input now shows matching securities (code + name) from loaded portfolio data,
  clicking a match fills in the correct code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add nodemailer dependency for email API route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(models): behebe Scroll-zurück-hoch-Problem auf der Modellseite (#1168)

- Füge `min-h-0` zu PullToRefresh in ModelsCatalog hinzu, damit der
  Scroll-Container korrekt auf die Viewport-Höhe beschränkt wird.
  Ohne `min-h-0` expandierte der flex-1-Container auf die volle
  Content-Höhe, weshalb das Scrollen im AppShell-overflow-y-auto
  stattfand. PullToRefresh.scrollTop blieb dadurch immer 0, sodass
  jeder Aufwärts-Swipe fälschlicherweise Pull-to-Refresh auslöste.

- Ändere `scrollTop === 0` zu `scrollTop <= 1` in PullToRefresh.tsx
  für robusteres Verhalten bei Subpixel-Werten durch Momentum-Scrolling
  (-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01D6ZXm3C1AjTgSipbULHTzW

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use body[actions] delta instead of stale body[state][values] for checkbox merge

body["state"]["values"] contains ALL blocks' checkbox states from the clicking
user's client render, which can be stale when multiple users interact quickly.

Bug: A checks 5 boxes → B sees all 5 synced → B clicks 1 more box
→ state.values for other blocks has empty selected_options (B's stale render)
→ Step 3 removes all of A's 5 items, Step 4 adds only B's 1 → A's checks lost

Fix: use body["actions"] which contains ONLY the interacted block with reliable
selected_options. Merge strategy: ih state (authoritative for all blocks) +
actions delta (replace only the changed block) = correct multi-user state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip leading silence before classification and preview

Both the CLAP classifier and the browser preview now scan up to 30s
to find the first non-silent region (RMS > -40 dBFS), then use
max_duration seconds from that onset rather than from frame 0.

- classify_audio.py load_audio(): re-loads with librosa offset= when
  onset is >0.5s in, so CLAP always hears real instrument content
- progress_server.py _audio_preview(): same scan logic, same fix

commit.message:
Fix dealio_mt4trades full sync for 10M rows — keyset pagination

Replace cursor.fetchmany() with TOP N + WHERE ticket > last_ticket pagination.
pymssql may buffer the full result set client-side, which would OOM on 10M rows.
Keyset pagination on the clustered PK guarantees max 50k rows per query.

Use trust_remote_code=True: qwen3_5_moe not in transformers 4.52.4

AutoConfig raises KeyError for 'qwen3_5_moe' because the architecture
was not backported to 4.52.4. Since upgrading transformers is not an
option, use trust_remote_code=True so the model loads its own
architecture code from the Hub cache. Removed all AutoConfig /
PretrainedConfig patching (no longer needed with remote code).

feat: add ThoughtSpot TML to Lightdash translation skill (#20852)

* feat: add ThoughtSpot TML to Lightdash translation skill

New skill that teaches Claude how to translate ThoughtSpot TML exports
into Lightdash YAML files (charts-as-code and dashboards-as-code).

Covers Liveboards → dashboards, Answers → charts, Worksheets → model
YAML with metrics/dimensions. Includes chart type mapping, filter
operator mapping, formula translation patterns, and a full worked
example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve ThoughtSpot TML skill accuracy and completeness

- Add Model TML v2 support (ThoughtSpot is deprecating Worksheets)
- Fix filter operator mapping to handle both symbol and enum formats
- Fix formula-to-metric example to use model-level meta.metrics
  instead of fake column (which wouldn't work in dbt projects)
- Add search_query parsing guide with keyword reference
- Add unsupported chart type mappings (sankey, candlestick, etc.)
- Add headline/KPI visualization handling
- Add Known Gaps table documenting untranslatable features
- Add fqn and client_state handling guidance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve YAML structure bugs and improve TML skill accuracy

- Fix duplicate meta: key on fact_sales model (invalid YAML) by merging
  joins and formula metrics into a single meta block
- Fix chart YAML filters to use explicit structure with dimensions/metrics/tableCalculations arrays
- Add note that joins_with is preferred over joins for sql_on translation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve ThoughtSpot TML skill accuracy against official docs and Lightdash schema

Cross-referenced ThoughtSpot TML skill against official ThoughtSpot documentation
and the Lightdash chart-as-code TypeScript types (packages/common/src/types/coder.ts).

ThoughtSpot TML fixes:
- Fix join type: OUTER not FULL_OUTER (per official docs)
- Fix filter operators: lowercase symbols (=, !=, in, not in) not uppercase enums (EQ, NE)
- Fix axis_configs structure: uses {column_id: "Name"} objects, not bare strings
- Add 9 missing chart types: GEO_HEATMAP, GEO_EARTH_*, GRID_TABLE, STACKED_BAR
- Remove non-existent chart types: DONUT, GAUGE, KPI, HEADLINE (not in enum)
- Add missing aggregation types: NONE, STD_DEVIATION, VARIANCE
- Add missing cardinality values for joins_with: MANY_TO_ONE, ONE_TO_ONE, ONE_TO_MANY
- Add missing column properties: is_attribution_dimension, is_additive, data_type values
- Document pinboard vs liveboard top-level key (legacy vs current)
- Add headline visualization docs (display_headline_column, not a chart type)
- Add tile size field and parameter_overrides to Liveboard schema
- Remove non-existent source field from joins_with

Lightdash output fixes:
- Fix chart filters: use FilterGroup ({and: [...]}) not flat arrays
- Fix dashboard filters: omit id (auto-generated), add required label field
- Add uuid and tileSlug fields to dashboard tiles
- Use map chart type for GEO_AREA/GEO_BUBBLE/GEO_HEATMAP instead of table
- Fix pie chart config to include isDonut field
- Remove reference to non-existent developing-in-lightdash skill
- Add slug pattern requirement (^[a-z0-9-]+$)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve ThoughtSpot TML to Lightdash translation skill accuracy

Cross-referenced against official ThoughtSpot docs and Lightdash
chart-as-code JSON schema to fix multiple inaccuracies:

ThoughtSpot TML fixes:
- Fix Model TML structure: uses `model_tables:` (not `tables:`),
  `columns:` (not `model_columns:`), and inline joins with `with:`/
  `on:`/`cardinality:` instead of top-level joins sections
- Fix filter operators to match official docs: `in`, `not in`,
  `between`, `=<`, `!=`, `<=`, `>=`, `>`, `<` (not `=`, `contains`,
  `begins with`, etc. which are search keywords not TML operators)
- Fix Liveboard TML top-level key: `pinboard:` is the historical
  default, `liveboard:` is used in newer exports
- Fix join type: `OUTER` not `FULL_OUTER` in TML
- Fix `joins_with` uses `is_one_to_one` boolean, not `cardinality`
- Remove `UNIQUE_COUNT` aggregation (not in official docs)
- Add `parameters` section docs with `range_config`/`list_config`
- Add `headline_aggregation` values including `TABLE_AGGR`
- Add complete formula field docs (`id`, `was_auto_generated`, etc.)

Lightdash schema fixes:
- Fix required chart fields: only `name`, `chartConfig`, `slug`,
  `tableName`, `spaceSlug`, `version`, `metricQuery` are required
- Fix required dashboard fields: only `name`, `slug`, `spaceSlug`,
  `version`, `tiles`, `tabs` are required
- Fix dashboard filter: `label` is optional, not required
- Add missing chart types: `gauge`, `custom`
- Add missing tile types: `sql_chart`, `loom`, `heading`
- Add `pivotConfig` as optional chart field
- Add Lightdash date-specific filter operators
- Note `exploreName` is required in metricQuery

Known gaps additions:
- `lesson_plans`, `range_config`/`list_config` parameters,
  `index_type`/`index_priority`, `spotiq_preference`,
  `is_attribution_dimension`/`is_additive`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: final pass — cross-reference skill files against Lightdash schemas

Verified all 5 skill files against chart-as-code and dashboard-as-code JSON
schemas and TypeScript types. Key fixes:

- Add pie chart groupFieldIds/metricId to examples and chart type mapping
- Document is_mandatory → required and excluded_visualizations → tileTargets mappings
- Add heading tile example with correct `text` property
- Document disabled, required, singleValue optional dashboard filter fields
- Add big_number label field to headline example
- Expand Known Gaps with verified schema details for pie config, cartesian
  metadata, tileTargets structure, and big_number additional config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verified all 5 skill files against chart-as-code-1.0.json, dashboard-as-code-1.0.json,
and TypeScript types (ChartAsCode, DashboardAsCode, MetricQuery, FilterRuleInput, etc.)

Fixes:
- Add required metricQuery fields documentation (exploreName, dimensions, metrics,

commit.message:
Add Parseable Cloud CTA, adopters section, auto-sync, and frictionless adopter registration (#1569)

- Update README description to link Parseable Cloud (app.parseable.com)
- Add centered Cloud CTA block before YouTube embed
- Add Adopters section (between Features and Verify Images) with
  ADOPTERS:START/END markers, sourced from USERS.md
- Add GitHub Action to auto-sync adopters from USERS.md to README
  on push to main
- frictionless adopter registration via GitHub Issue Form

How it works:
1. User opens an issue using the "Add Adopter" template
2. Fills in: Organization Name, URL, Contact, and Description of Use
3. A GitHub Action automatically validates the submission, checks for
   duplicates, and creates a PR updating USERS.md
4. Maintainers review and merge the PR — issue auto-closes
